quizQuestion:
J'aime le café, {___} je préfère le thé le soir.
(I like coffee, but I prefer tea in the evening.)
sentenceTranslation: I like coffee, but I prefer tea in the evening.
explanation: mais (but) introduces a contrast or opposition between two ideas.

quizQuestion:
Il pleuvait, {___} nous sommes restés à la maison.
(It was raining, so we stayed home.)
sentenceTranslation: It was raining, so we stayed home.
explanation: donc (so / therefore) introduces a logical consequence from the preceding clause.

quizQuestion:
Je suis fatigué {___} j'ai travaillé toute la nuit.
(I'm tired because I worked all night.)
sentenceTranslation: I'm tired because I worked all night.
explanation: parce que (because) introduces the cause or reason for the main clause and is followed by a full subject-verb clause.

quizQuestion:
Il lisait {___} elle cuisinait.
(He was reading while she was cooking.)
sentenceTranslation: He was reading while she was cooking.
explanation: pendant que (while) connects two simultaneous actions happening over the same period.

quizQuestion:
{___} j'étais enfant, j'habitais à Lyon.
(When I was a child, I lived in Lyon.)
sentenceTranslation: When I was a child, I lived in Lyon.
explanation: quand (when) introduces a time clause situating the action of the main clause.

quizQuestion:
Je prends un manteau {___} il fait froid.
(I'm taking a coat because it is cold.)
sentenceTranslation: I'm taking a coat because it is cold.
explanation: car (for / because) gives a reason, similar to parce que but more formal and never used to start a sentence.

quizQuestion:
J'ai pris mon petit-déjeuner, {___} je suis sorti.
(I had breakfast, then I went out.)
sentenceTranslation: I had breakfast, then I went out.
explanation: puis (then / next) sequences events in chronological order, typically in narration.

quizQuestion:
Il n'avait pas de clé, {___} il a appelé un serrurier.
(He had no key, so he called a locksmith.)
sentenceTranslation: He had no key, so he called a locksmith.
explanation: alors (so / then) marks a consequence or the next step in a narrative sequence.

quizQuestion:
D'abord on visite le musée, {___} on déjeune au restaurant.
(First we visit the museum, then we have lunch at the restaurant.)
sentenceTranslation: First we visit the museum, then we have lunch at the restaurant.
explanation: ensuite (then / afterwards) indicates the next step in a sequence, often paired with d'abord.

quizQuestion:
{___}, il faut prendre le métro, puis changer à Châtelet.
(First, you take the metro, then change at Châtelet.)
sentenceTranslation: First, you take the metro, then change at Châtelet.
explanation: d'abord (first / first of all) opens an ordered sequence and typically precedes ensuite or puis.

quizQuestion:
Il a répondu très {___} à ma question.
(He answered my question very quickly.)
sentenceTranslation: He answered my question very quickly.
explanation: rapidement (quickly) is formed from rapide → feminine rapide + -ment (adjective ends in -e, no change needed).

quizQuestion:
Le professeur parle {___} pour que tout le monde comprenne.
(The teacher speaks slowly so that everyone understands.)
sentenceTranslation: The teacher speaks slowly so that everyone understands.
explanation: lentement (slowly) is formed from lent → feminine lente + -ment.

quizQuestion:
Il a frappé {___} à la porte pour ne pas réveiller les enfants.
(He knocked gently on the door so as not to wake the children.)
sentenceTranslation: He knocked gently on the door so as not to wake the children.
explanation: doucement (gently / softly) is formed from doux → feminine douce + -ment.

quizQuestion:
J'aime {___} nager dans la mer.
(I really like swimming in the sea.)
sentenceTranslation: I really like swimming in the sea.
explanation: vraiment (really / truly) is formed from vrai → feminine vraie + -ment.

quizQuestion:
Elle apprend les langues {___}.
(She learns languages easily.)
sentenceTranslation: She learns languages easily.
explanation: facilement (easily) is formed from facile → feminine facile + -ment (adjective ends in -e, suffix appended directly).

quizQuestion:
Ce texte se lit {___} sans dictionnaire.
(This text is read with difficulty without a dictionary.)
sentenceTranslation: This text is read with difficulty without a dictionary.
explanation: difficilement (with difficulty) is formed from difficile → feminine difficile + -ment.

quizQuestion:
{___}, personne n'a été blessé dans l'accident.
(Fortunately, nobody was hurt in the accident.)
sentenceTranslation: Fortunately, nobody was hurt in the accident.
explanation: heureusement (fortunately) is formed from heureux → feminine heureuse + -ment.

quizQuestion:
C'est {___} vrai.
(It is unfortunately true.)
sentenceTranslation: It is unfortunately true.
explanation: malheureusement (unfortunately) is formed from malheureux → feminine malheureuse + -ment.

quizQuestion:
J'ai pris froid {___}.
(I caught a cold yesterday.)
sentenceTranslation: I caught a cold yesterday.
explanation: hier (yesterday) is a time marker that triggers the passé composé for a completed action.

quizQuestion:
Il est arrivé {___}, pas hier.
(He arrived the day before yesterday, not yesterday.)
sentenceTranslation: He arrived the day before yesterday, not yesterday.
explanation: avant-hier (the day before yesterday) refers to two days ago and is used with passé composé.

quizQuestion:
Nous avons visité le Louvre {___}.
(We visited the Louvre last week.)
sentenceTranslation: We visited the Louvre last week.
explanation: la semaine dernière (last week) specifies a completed past period and collocates with the passé composé.

quizQuestion:
Ils ont déménagé à Paris {___}.
(They moved to Paris last year.)
sentenceTranslation: They moved to Paris last year.
explanation: l'année dernière (last year) denotes a completed past year and is used with the passé composé.

quizQuestion:
J'ai appris à conduire {___}.
(I learned to drive two years ago.)
sentenceTranslation: I learned to drive two years ago.
grammarPointLabel: il y a + duration — Time expression (ago)
explanation: il y a + duration (two years ago) indicates elapsed time since a past event and is used with passé composé.

quizQuestion:
J'habite ici {___} trois ans.
(I have been living here for three years.)
sentenceTranslation: I have been living here for three years.
grammarPointLabel: depuis — Ongoing duration from the past
explanation: depuis (since / for) with the present tense expresses a situation that started in the past and is still ongoing.

quizQuestion:
J'attends {___} des heures !
(I have been waiting for hours!)
sentenceTranslation: I have been waiting for hours!
explanation: depuis (for) with present tense shows an action still in progress; the waiting started in the past and continues now.

quizQuestion:
J'ai dormi {___} neuf heures.
(I slept for nine hours.)
sentenceTranslation: I slept for nine hours.
explanation: pendant (for) with passé composé expresses a completed duration; the sleeping is finished.

quizQuestion:
J'ai lu {___} une heure avant de dormir.
(I read for an hour before sleeping.)
sentenceTranslation: I read for an hour before sleeping.
explanation: pendant (for) marks a closed, finished span of time used with passé composé or imparfait.

quizQuestion:
Je l'ai rencontré {___} une semaine.
(I met him a week ago.)
sentenceTranslation: I met him a week ago.
explanation: il y a + duration (ago) measures time elapsed since a completed past event; used with passé composé.

quizQuestion:
Le train part {___} dix minutes.
(The train leaves in ten minutes.)
sentenceTranslation: The train leaves in ten minutes.
explanation: dans + duration (in X time) points forward from the present moment to a future event, the opposite of il y a.

quizQuestion:
C'est l'homme {___} habite en face de chez moi.
(That's the man who lives across from me.)
sentenceTranslation: That's the man who lives across from me.
explanation: qui (who / which) is the relative pronoun used when the antecedent is the subject of the relative clause.

quizQuestion:
Ceux {___} le connaissent l'apprécient.
(Those who know him like him.)
sentenceTranslation: Those who know him like him.
explanation: qui acts as the subject of the relative clause and can refer to people or things.

quizQuestion:
C'est le film {___} j'ai vu hier soir.
(That's the film that I watched last night.)
sentenceTranslation: That's the film that I watched last night.
explanation: que (that / which / whom) is used when the antecedent is the direct object of the relative clause.

quizQuestion:
C'est l'ami {___} elle a rencontré à Paris.
(That's the friend that she met in Paris.)
sentenceTranslation: That's the friend that she met in Paris.
grammarPointLabel: qu' — Elision of que before vowel
explanation: que elides to qu' before a word beginning with a vowel or silent h; the function (direct object) remains the same.

quizQuestion:
Le livre {___} je lis est très intéressant.
(The book that I am reading is very interesting.)
sentenceTranslation: The book that I am reading is very interesting.
explanation: que links the relative clause where le livre is the direct object of lire; the subject je follows immediately after que.

quizQuestion:
J'ai un chat {___} adore dormir sur le canapé.
(I have a cat that loves sleeping on the sofa.)
sentenceTranslation: I have a cat that loves sleeping on the sofa.
explanation: qui can refer to animals and inanimate things as well as people when it functions as the subject of the relative clause.
